======= RMM01859Barry family papers1859Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Barry family papers
Barry family.
1859
.3 cubic feet. .3 cubic feet.
1793-1956. Account books, correspondence, deeds, a justice dockett, trade cards, and other papers. Papers of the Charles H. Barry family of Pittstown, Rensselaer County, New York, include family, store, and blacksmith account books, 1806-79; a few personal letters and deeds; justice dockett, 1841-63; report on the condition of the Troy Turnpike, ca.1867; trade cards; and miscellaneous papers. Collection material in English 2078617
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, March 2003
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Papers of the Charles H. Barry family of Pittstown, Rensselaer County, New York, include family, store, and blacksmith account books, 1806-79; a few personal letters and deeds; justice dockett, 1841-63; report on the condition of the Troy Turnpike, ca.1867; trade cards; and miscellaneous papers.
Cite As:
Barry family papers, #1859. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Troy Turnpike. Trade cards. Dockets. Deeds. Account books. Rennselaer County (N.Y.) Pittstown (N.Y.) Blacksmiths. Toll roads. Barry, Charles H.
2078617 <<<<<<< HEAD Indenture made Mar. 7, 1793 between Lavinus Lansing and Addi Bigelow: receipts and transferals from Apr. 3, 1795-Sept. 11, 1801 written on back; land involved in Rensselaer County, NY179311Figures for dates June 30 and Aug. 7, 1801 refer to new shoes180112Smith Filkins to DLF Caw, Oct. 11, 1814 (1845?) Raymertown, Rensselaer County, refers to yard goods181412Counsellor at Law license for Andrew Thompson signed by Smith Thompson, Chief Justice of New York State, Jan. 15, 1817181712Summons to Seneca Bratt, Mar. 1, 1845, signed Charles H. Barry, Justice of the Peace184512Receipt of John Downey, Oct. 12, 1849, received by I.H. Barry (his committee), signed by John S. Van Valkenbergh (?) and John C. Mather (?), Commissioner of Loans, Troy, NY184912General bills dated Sept. 5, 1852 and Jan. 22, 1853185212Letter from H. Filkins (?) to his father, Mar. 28, 1853, Niagara Falls, NY, mentions "Mr. Barry"185312Letter from Jno. S Barry to his brother Charles H. Barry, Apr. 18, 1853,New York City, concerning a loan185312Sept. 5, 1852 and Aug. 1, 1853 bills to Abijah Downing and John Downing for carpentry185312Mr. Filkins to D.L.J. Caw, Sept. 4, 1854, in regard to tailoring185412Receipt signed by Charles H. Barry Jr., Apr. 13, 1858, acknowledginh payment by Jno. S. Barry, Constantine, Michigan185813Deed search, recorded May 10, 1859, mortgage last assigned to John S. Barry, originally land given to John Waltham - no town mentioned185913Summons, Dec. 16, 1862, signed Chas. H. Barry, Justice Peace, Pittstown, New York, against Mary E. Stover186213Statement by Chas. H. Barry certifying the recording of the mortgage of 1859, Oct. 17, 1863, Rensselaer County186313Letter to Chas. H. Barry from Jno. S. Barry, May 3, 1864, Constantine, Michigan186413Indenture, Sept. 24, 1864, Leonidas A. Barry and wife to Chas. H. Barry, recorded Jan. 26, 1866, Rensselaer County186613Promisory note, May 8, 1866, signed Chas. H. Barry to pay $518.29 to Cornelia Francisco186613Application for the Alteration of a Highway - C.H. Barry, Mar. 23, 1867, addressed to Benjamin I. Carpenter and Alpha Hayner186713Statement report concerning road conditions of Troy Turnpike, Apr. 6, 1867 (first draft? no signatures)186713Indenture made Sept. 26, 1868, between David Akin and Harmon Cole and Charles H. Barry186814Receipt of court costs from Charles H. Barry signed on Dec. 18, 1868 by J. Romeyn, Defendant's Attorney, in the case of Hiram Slocum against Chas. H. Barry186814Letter from Chas. H. Barry to his father, Jan. 12, 1871, Constantine, Michigan187114Certification-discharge of mortgage of Joseph Codd to C.H. Barry, Oct. 4, 1883, Saginaw County, Michigan188314Letter to Nelson McChesney, Pittstown, New York, from T.J.O. Hare, 16 Pearl St., New York City, July 24, 1885188515Letter to C.H.Barry, Raymertown, New York, from Chas. H. Barry Jr., Constantine, Michigan, May 19, 1890189015Letter from C.H. Barry to his brother, Constantine, Michigan, June 30, 1891189115Receipt for digging grave of Lucy Button Estate, signed by Elmer E Hayner (Keeper of grounds), Pittstown, Nov. 9, 1891189115Printed to "Brother Philo" concerning a reunion of the Philo Organization signed by Burt Matteson (printed), Fairfield, New York, Feb. 19, 1894189415Letter to John S. Barry from Charles I. Webster, Troy, New York, May 26, 1900190015Fractional Warrant - The Missouri Pacific Railway Company, Dec. 9, 1909, New York City, for Helen B. Markham190915Letter Chas. H. Barry from C. H. Barry (Jr.), Constantine, Michigan (envelope dated Mar. 27, 1911)191115Letter to Mr. Ball from Chas. H. Barry, Raymertown, New York, Sept. 4, 1911191115Letter (incomplete) to "Dear Cousins" from ? (signature missing); assume the letter was written by Chas. H. Barry because it is from Raymertown191115Letter from "Cupcake" to ?, Feb. 16, 1956 on Hotel Bristol, Cairo stationary (she was not there)195615Card announcing 100th celebration dinner of birth of Alexander Humboldt - Charles H. Barrie written on back16Memo - no date - a list of names - none of which have been mentioned above16Letter from Charles Barry to Santa Claus - no date16Memo concerning funeral costs - no date - on back of Manufacturer's National Bank, Troy, New York, deposit slip16Prescription from Eaton J. Gross, Veterinary Surgeon, Troy, New York, no date16Notes on Greek sculpture and a listing of other periosds written on Fort Edward (N.Y.) Collegiate Institute stationary16"A Baby's letter" - a poem (?) by Baby to "dear old uncle"16Incomplete Bond stating debt of Hugh McChesney to Chas. H. Barry, both of Pittstown, New York163 printed lists of candidates for various state and town offices166 paper fragments from blacksmith account book with miscellaneous figures and names as follows: Joseph Foster, Simon V. Cook, James Robert, John Downing, S amuel Shiffer, John Shiffer, William Dunnby; on refers to a court case, mentions Herman Knickerbacker v. John Sam16Account book of merchant - notations made of large quantities of silk, cotton goods, flour, coffee and occassionally bank stock which her bought ans sold. Also general summaries of cash at hand and expenditures concerning his store - Jan. 1, 1859-Jan. 1, 1869. Back pages filled with personnal accounts of farmer, (?), May 1871-Nov. 27, 18721859-6917Court record book of Chas. H. Barry - recording of damages and costs of various court cases over which he presided. Also includes a brief summary of each case - Feb. 23, 1841-Decenber 3, 1863. A lsit of his mother's aunts and uncles in back of book (Filkins and Van Dercook's)1841-6318Records of a personal account, apparently of amounts owed and paid to various firms and individuals. Barry's name mentioned several times near end of book - Mar. 12, 1847-Aug. 19, 1854. Some entries for 1856, but in different hand1847-5419Pittstown Journal - "1830" - but actually 1833 - July 20-Oct. 30, 18331806-57110A family account book - Apr. 1, 1873-Mar. 4, 18791873-79111Account book of blacksmith (?) makes frequent mention of shoe sets, hammers and mending of steel goods. Later on in book many notations refer to farm labor work and handy-man jobs - Apr. 16, 1806-Oct. 25, 1857 (worked for Charles H. Barry in year of 1840)1806-57112 ======= Indenture made Mar. 7, 1793 between Lavinus Lansing and Addi Bigelow: receipts and transferals from Apr. 3, 1795-Sept. 11, 1801 written on back; land involved in Rensselaer County, NY179311Figures for dates June 30 and Aug. 7, 1801 refer to new shoes180112Smith Filkins to DLF Caw, Oct. 11, 1814 (1845?) Raymertown, Rensselaer County, refers to yard goods181412Counsellor at Law license for Andrew Thompson signed by Smith Thompson, Chief Justice of New York State, Jan. 15, 1817181712Summons to Seneca Bratt, Mar. 1, 1845, signed Charles H. Barry, Justice of the Peace184512Receipt of John Downey, Oct. 12, 1849, received by I.H. Barry (his committee), signed by John S. Van Valkenbergh (?) and John C. Mather (?), Commissioner of Loans, Troy, NY184912General bills dated Sept. 5, 1852 and Jan. 22, 1853185212Letter from H. Filkins (?) to his father, Mar. 28, 1853, Niagara Falls, NY, mentions "Mr. Barry"185312Letter from Jno. S Barry to his brother Charles H.
